ucs.title: [LLM] Ferocious Kitten RTLO-disguised executable masquerading as Persian Office/PDF lure
ucs.description: Hunts files and process executions whose names contain the U+202E Right-to-Left Override character — a hallmark Ferocious Kitten trick used to flip a trailing 'cod.scr/exe' so a Persian-speaking victim sees a benign-looking .doc/.pdf in the file. Combined with execution from user-writable paths typical of their spearphish ZIPs.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\Downloads\\*","*\\Desktop\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id _time span=1h | rename Processes.* as * | regex process_name="\x{202E}" OR process_path="\x{202E}" | eval rtlo_hit=if(match(process_name,"\x{202E}") OR match(process_path,"\x{202E}"),1,0) | where rtlo_hit=1 OR like(process,"%\u202E%") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Ferocious Kitten - U+202E (RTLO) filename masquerade in delivery chain
let rtlo = "\u202E";
union
(DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName has rtlo or FolderPath has rtlo or PreviousFileName has rtlo
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\", @"\Desktop\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, FileName, FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SHA256),
(DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName has rtlo or ProcessCommandLine has rtlo or FolderPath has rtlo
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, AccountName, SHA256)
| extend HasRTLO = true
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Kaspersky's 2021 reporting on Ferocious Kitten (Iranian state-aligned, Persian-speaking targets) explicitly documents the RTLO trick to render dropper EXEs as legitimate-looking Persian documents in spearphish bait. The U+202E codepoint in a filename is rare in benign telemetry, making this a high-fidelity pivot specifically tied to FK's MarkiRAT/DroidRAT delivery — versus a generic T1036 rule tha

ucs.title: [LLM] Ferocious Kitten MarkiRAT persistence via hijacked Telegram Desktop / fake Chrome updater
ucs.description: Detects MarkiRAT's signature persistence: replacing/sideloading into the Telegram Desktop install directory under %APPDATA%\Telegram Desktop\ and dropping a fake 'GoogleUpdate.exe' / 'Update.exe' that hijacks the user's launch of Telegram or Chrome. This combo (Telegram-themed living-off-the-bag + Chrome-update masquerade) is uniquely associated with Ferocious Kitten's Iran-targeted operations.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\Telegram Desktop\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\*\\Google\\Update\\*") (Filesystem.file_name IN ("GoogleUpdate.exe","Update.exe","Telegram.exe","svchost.exe","chrome_updater.exe")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name | rename Filesystem.* as * | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=Telegram.exe OR Processes.process_name=GoogleUpdate.exe) Processes.parent_process_name!=explorer.exe Processes.parent_process_name!=services.exe by Processes.dest Processes.process_name] | where isnotnull(cmdline) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.rationale: Kaspersky's analysis of MarkiRAT (Ferocious Kitten's flagship implant) shows it specifically replaces the Telegram Desktop launcher in %APPDATA%\Telegram Desktop\ — exploiting Telegram's popularity with Iranian dissidents — and drops a 'GoogleUpdate.exe' decoy to abuse Chrome's auto-update folder structure. Genuine GoogleUpdate.exe lives under %LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Update\ and is signed/launched b
